page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon CPU with 40 physical cores and 80 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz. It features 320 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes 33 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 75,092.52 and a PassMark CPU score of 52,649.44. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, registering 213.45 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 94.1 GB/sec for write operations. Database operations are a notable strength, with Redis SET operations reaching 63.15 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring 22,522.26.

While multi-threaded execution and memory throughput are strong, single-core performance remains average, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1,201.0. Additionally, without hardware acceleration, the server is poorly suited for large-scale language model text generation, with 70B model text generation dropping to 2.55 tokens per second. The qualitative cost efficiency of this server lies in its high resource density and balanced CPU-to-memory ratio, making it suitable for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 80 vCPUs across 40 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 320 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks demonstrate strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 75,092.52 and high memory bandwidth, including 213.45 GB/sec for large-block reads. Database performance is also strong, highlighted by 63.15 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core performance is average, and text generation for large 70B language models is poor at 2.55 tokens per second due to the absence of dedicated accelerators. This server provides qualitative cost efficiency for high-density, multi-threaded workloads, making it suitable for software compilation, database management, and enterprise web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 is an 80-vCPU Intel Xeon server with 320 GB RAM. It delivers strong multi-core and database performance but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-80 features 80 vCPUs and 320 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and compilation.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose server featuring eighty virtual cores and balanced memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 80 vCPUs and 320 GB RAM on Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture
Strong multi-core performance and high memory read/write bandwidth
Excellent database throughput with 63.15 million Redis SET operations per second
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Poorly suited for large-scale LLM text generation
Suited for software compilation, database hosting, and multi-threaded applications
